variance_explanation: Two major deals slipped to February: Acme Corp $120K (procurement delayed) and TechStart $80K (budget freeze). Timing-related variance.
Unexpected enterprise deal closed early (GlobalTech $45K). Strong Q1 performance.
Implementation project delayed by 2 weeks due to customer resource constraints.
Higher contractor costs due to skill shortage in cloud migration projects.
Unplanned trade show participation ($18K) and additional headcount hired in December.
Lower commissions due to revenue miss. Directly correlated to deal slippage.
Digital campaign overspend ($7K) to support Q1 pipeline generation.
Cloud infrastructure costs increased 4% due to higher-than-expected user growth.
Emergency security patch deployment and additional AWS costs.
Slipped deals from January closed (Acme $120K + TechStart $80K) plus new deals.

Major deal pushed to Q2 due to regulatory approval delays in China ($90K).
Increased customer visits and partner conference attendance.
Unplanned audit fees for SOX compliance review.
Q1 delayed China deal closed ($90K) plus strong new pipeline execution.
New product launch infrastructure costs and increased headcount.
Economic uncertainty in UK market impacting deal velocity.
Major industry conference sponsorship and product launch campaign.
Strong start to FY2025 with enterprise pipeline conversion exceeding plan.
New sales territory expansion and additional headcount.
Japan market softness and delayed government sector deals.
AI/ML platform development acceleration and cloud compute costs.

Two major orders slipped to February: Acme Corp $120K (procurement approval delayed) and TechStart $80K (customer capital freeze). Timing-related variance, not structural.
GlobalTech framework order closed 3 weeks early ($42K). Strong Q1 pipeline execution.
Steel plate prices rose 14% due to EU tariff changes. $23K unbudgeted raw material overspend. Procurement reviewing alternative suppliers.
Unplanned Hannover Messe sponsorship ($18K) approved late. Additional headcount in December not in original plan.
Lower commissions directly correlated to $175K revenue miss in NA Plant. Timing-related — expected to recover in February.
Digital campaign overspend ($7K) to support Q1 manufacturing pipeline generation amid revenue softness.
NA Plant implementation project delayed 2 weeks due to customer resource constraints. Revenue deferred to February.
January-slipped deals closed: Acme Corp $120K + TechStart $80K + additional spot orders $70K. Strong Feb recovery.
Steel tariff impact continues. Alternate supplier qualification in progress — expect normalisation by Q2.
China regulatory delay: Type-A certification renewal pushed shipment of $90K order to Q2 2026. Structural risk if resolution exceeds 60 days.
